event: RWA YIELD TOKEN accrues ABOVE $1 by design — FP-discipline + sign-direction control (USYC, dated 2025-06-01). USYC (Hashnote US Yield Coin) 0x136471a34f6ef19fE571EFFC1CA711fdb8E49f2b (6 dp, Ethereum) is the on-chain form of Hashnote's Short Duration Yield Fund (short US T-bills + reverse-repo). Yield is NOT a coupon/rebase: the fund NAV accrues daily and the USYC token price tracks NAV (price = NAV/supply, published on-chain via a Chainlink-interface oracle), so USYC LEGITIMATELY trades ABOVE $1 and redeems at NAV. MEASURED on-chain @ Ethereum block 22606143 (first block >= 2025-06-01T00:00:00Z): the USYC/USD NAV oracle 0x4c48bcb2160F8e0aDbf9D4F3B034f1e36d1f8b3e (8dp, Chainlink aggregator interface, description 'USYC / USD') reports answer = 1.08863422 (the 18dp sibling oracle 0x74f2199AEb743f68f05943e5715A33EaF2b61f53 agrees: 1.0886342286), last published 2025-05-30 (the daily ~9am ET NAV report; 2025-06-01 is a Sunday). So the MEASURED NAV is ~$1.0886 (+8.86% above $1), replacing the earlier representative ~$1.127. The benign premise is PRESERVED: the token trades ABOVE $1 by design, an UPWARD excursion of a COLLATERAL token (worth MORE than par) — the SAFE direction that raises over-collateralization and lowers liquidation risk; only a DOWNWARD break threatens lenders. CASE 1 (correct modeling): peg=NAV(measured 1.08863422) -> price==peg -> no deviation -> fully SILENT. CASE 2 (naive $1 peg): a +8.86% UPWARD excursion SHOULD surface a WARN config-hygiene review-nudge but must NEVER produce a catastrophic HIGH/CRITICAL page. Pure FP-discipline; depeg severity must be sign-aware. See EVENT.md.

Snapshot snapshots/usyc.json (host-fed graph). MEASURED price: USYC/USD NAV oracle latestRoundData() read on-chain @ ethereum:22606143 = 1.08863422 (fidelity measured=true; see snapshots/fidelity.json). See EVENT.md.

must_not_fire.why: CORRECT MODELING. USYC is a yield-bearing RWA whose NAV accrues above $1 by design (MEASURED on-chain ~$1.0886 @ ethereum:22606143), and the correct peg for such a token IS its NAV. Here the peg is set to the NAV (peg = price = 1.08863422, the measured oracle answer), so the deviation is EXACTLY ZERO — there is no peg to break. A DEPEG signal must therefore stay SILENT. This is the right way to model an accruing T-bill token: a flat-'$1' scan that called this 'off-peg' is the false positive being guarded against. Any DEPEG emission on this edge (at any severity) is wrong because price==peg.

cases.note: CONTROL / CORRECT-MODELING. snapshot snapshots/usyc.json, no prev, now 2025-06-01T00:00:00Z. peg_probes model the peg as the NAV (price 1.08863422 == peg 1.08863422, asset_class rwa, by_design_discount false). The price is MEASURED on-chain: USYC/USD NAV oracle 0x4c48bcb2160F8e0aDbf9D4F3B034f1e36d1f8b3e latestRoundData() @ ethereum:22606143 = 1.08863422 (fidelity measured=true). Asserts the noise ceiling: ZERO WARN-or-above (INFO housekeeping tolerable). When the accruing RWA's peg is correctly its NAV there is no deviation at all, so the monitor must be fully silent — proving the depeg path does not false-fire on a token that trades above $1 by design. Backfill: only the price/peg input swapped modelled 1.127 -> measured 1.08863422; the judgment (silent) is unchanged because price==peg keeps deviation at exactly zero.

should_fire.why: EXPECTED REVIEW-NUDGE. With a NAIVE flat $1 peg set against the MEASURED price 1.08863422 (on-chain NAV @ ethereum:22606143), the collateral sits +8.86% off the $1 reference. That off-band excursion legitimately surfaces a DEPEG at WARN — 'trading 8.86% above the $1 reference, a NAV-accrual / peg-misconfig to review'. This WARN is the CORRECT, expected behavior, NOT a false positive: a config-hygiene nudge that asks an operator to look (the peg is likely misconfigured to a flat $1 instead of NAV, or the token is accruing as designed) without paging anyone. The discipline being locked in is the SEVERITY of that surfacing — a review-nudge (WARN), never a catastrophic liquidation page (HIGH/CRITICAL).

cases.note: INCIDENT-FAMILY EXPECTATION (closed=false) / SIGN-DIRECTION + CATASTROPHE-CEILING. snapshot snapshots/usyc.json, no prev, now 2025-06-01T00:00:00Z. peg_probes naively misconfigure the peg to a flat $1.00 while price is the MEASURED on-chain NAV 1.08863422 (USYC/USD oracle 0x4c48bcb2160F8e0aDbf9D4F3B034f1e36d1f8b3e @ ethereum:22606143) — a +8.86% UPWARD deviation of a yield-bearing RWA COLLATERAL token (asset_class rwa, by_design_discount false). With a NAIVE $1 peg, this upward +8.86% deviation SHOULD surface a DEPEG at WARN — a legitimate config-hygiene review-nudge ('trading 8.86% above the $1 reference — a NAV-accrual / peg-misconfig to review'). That WARN is the CORRECT expected behavior, NOT an FP, which is why this case is closed=false (a WARN SHOULD fire — an incident-family expectation, not a pure silent control). BUT max_emitted {HIGH, 0} = ZERO HIGH-or-above: the upward excursion must NEVER raise a catastrophic HIGH/CRITICAL liquidation page. An upward move means the collateral is worth MORE than par, which INCREASES over-collateralization and REDUCES liquidation risk — the SAFE direction; only a DOWNWARD break threatens lenders, so depeg severity must be sign-aware. should_fire demands the WARN; must_not_fire empty; only HIGH-or-above is forbidden. Load-bearing assertion: surface a WARN review-nudge on the upward move, but NEVER catastrophically page on a collateral token moving UP. beta 2.0. Backfill: only the price input swapped modelled 1.127 -> measured 1.08863422 (deviation +12.7% -> +8.86%); the judgment is unchanged because the move stays UPWARD and well within the by-design above-$1 accrual band — the benign/non-catastrophe premise is preserved at the measured value.
